findings.summary: Component counts are accurate: 38 total, 6 capacitors, 7 resistors, 2 ICs, 2 transistors, 3 LEDs, 3 connectors, 3 jumpers, 4 fiducials, 4 mounting holes; LDO regulator U1 (RT9080-1.8V) correctly de...

correct.description: The analyzer correctly extracted all 38 components from the schematic. Component type breakdown matches: C1-C6 (6 caps), R1-R5/R7/R8 (7 resistors with R6 absent by design), Q1-Q2 (2 NMOS), D1-D3 (3 LEDs), J1-J3 (3 connectors), JP1-JP3 (3 jumpers), FID1-FID4 (4 fiducials), ST1-ST4 (4 mounting holes), U1-U2 (2 ICs), G1-G4 (4 aesthetic symbols). DNP count of 1 (D3) is correct.

The analyzer correctly identifies U1 as an LDO regulator with input_rail=3.3V and output_rail=1.8V. The estimated_vout=1.8 derived from the part value suffix is correct. Decoupling caps C5/C2 on the input and C6/C4/C1 on the output are correctly identified.

The analyzer correctly identifies both 1.8V-side I2C lines (SDA_1V8 and SCL_1V8) as I2C buses with their respective 2.2k pull-up resistors R7 and R8 to the 1.8V rail. The level-shifted side connecting to U2 (AS7343) is correctly mapped.

R1 is a series 22-ohm resistor between the 1.8V LDO output and U2's VDD pin, with C3 (1.0uF) decoupling U2's VDD. The analyzer correctly computes the RC low-pass filter with fc = 7.23 kHz. The input_net=1.8V and output_net=__unnamed_1 (U2-VDD net) are correctly traced.

The analyzer correctly identifies three power rails (1.8V, 3.3V, GND) and reports total_nets=21. The net classification correctly labels 1.8V and 3.3V as power, GND as ground, SDA/SCL/SDA_1V8/SCL_1V8 as data/clock signals, GPIO as signal, and ~{INT} as interrupt.

D3 is a 5mm PTH red LED marked Do Not Populate (DNP=true) in the schematic. The analyzer correctly reports dnp_parts=1 and identifies D3 as dnp=true in the components list. All other 37 components are dnp=false.

G1 and G3 are schematic-only aesthetic symbols with empty footprint strings. The analyzer correctly identifies them in missing_footprint. G2 has a valid footprint (SparkFun_Logo_8mm) and G4 has a Creative_Commons_License footprint, so neither is in missing_footprint.

The 1.8V rail has C6 (4.7uF), C4 (4.7uF), C1 (0.1uF) for 9.5uF total; the 3.3V rail has C5 (4.7uF) and C2 (0.1uF) for 4.8uF total. Both have bulk and bypass caps correctly flagged. The absence of a high-frequency cap (e.g. 10nF or 100pF) is correctly noted as has_high_freq=false.

The schematic title block states 'SparkFun Spectral Sensor Breakout AS7353 Qwiic' but the component is AS7343. This is a typo in the source schematic. The analyzer faithfully extracts the title as written. The mismatch between the title and U2's actual value 'AS7343' is a noteworthy design metadata inconsistency.

check.value: SparkFun Spectral Sensor Breakout AS7353 Qwiic

correct.description: The analyzer correctly identifies U2's VDD connected through R1 (22 ohm) to the 1.8V filtered supply (__unnamed_1 net) with C3 local decoupling, SCL and SDA on the 1.8V level-shifted bus via Q1/Q2 BSS138 MOSFETs, INT with 10k pull-up (R2) to 1.8V, GPIO directly exposed to J3, and LDR (LED driver output) connected to D2/D3 via JP3 jumper. The subcircuit neighbor detection for U2 correctly includes R1, R2, R7, R8, Q1, Q2, C3, D3, JP3, J3.

incorrect.description: Q1 and Q2 are BSS138 N-MOSFETs used as bidirectional I2C level shifters. Their gates are tied directly to the 1.8V rail. R8 (for Q1) and R7 (for Q2) are 2.2k pull-up resistors on the SDA_1V8/SCL_1V8 nets (source side), not gate resistors. The analyzer incorrectly classifies them as gate_resistors because they share the 1.8V net with the gate, but they are electrically on the source/level-translated side. Similarly, U1 (LDO) is falsely listed as gate_driver_ic for Q1/Q2 — it merely provides the 1.8V rail that powers the gate bias.

missed.description: The design uses R4 (2.2k) on the SDA net and R5 (2.2k) on the SCL net, both connected through solder jumper JP2 to 3.3V. When JP2 is closed (default), R4 and R5 are 3.3V I2C pull-ups for the host-facing side. The bus_analysis.i2c only shows the 1.8V side (SDA_1V8, SCL_1V8); the 3.3V side SDA and SCL nets with their pull-ups are not detected as I2C bus segments. This is a missed detection since SDA and SCL nets connect to J1, J2 (Qwiic connectors) and J3 (PTH header).

findings.summary: PCB footprint counts, board dimensions, and routing status are accurate; GND copper zone correctly identified as spanning both layers with 56 stitching vias; Zone fill_ratio of 1.274 is incorrect —...

correct.description: footprint_count=64 (29 board-only + 29 SMD + 2 THT + 1 exclude_from_pos + 3 allow_soldermask_bridges), board dimensions 25.4x25.4mm, 2-layer design with F.Cu and B.Cu, 187 track segments, 77 vias, 1 GND copper zone, net_count=21, routing_complete=true, unrouted=0. All values verified against source PCB file.

The single copper zone is on GND net, spans both F.Cu and B.Cu, with zone area 645.16mm2 (matching 25.4x25.4mm board), is filled, and has 56 GND stitching vias. Thermal analysis correctly identifies the via stitching at 8.7 vias/cm2.

All 77 vias are through-hole type with size 0.56mm / 0.3mm drill, giving 0.13mm annular ring on each side. The analyzer correctly identifies uniform via sizing and reports them in the size_distribution. The current capacity estimate of 7.9A at 0.3mm drill is consistent with published via current tables.

U1 has closest cap at 3.17mm (C1), U2 has closest cap at 3.42mm (C3). Both are correctly identified as having shared nets with decoupling caps. The analysis correctly notes that C3 (1.0uF) on __unnamed_1 (U2-VDD) is the dedicated local decoupling for U2's filtered supply rail via R1.

156 segments at 0.2032mm, 14 at 0.4064mm, and 17 at 0.5588mm, with F.Cu having 133 segments and B.Cu having 54 segments. Total track length 295.16mm. These match the expected SparkFun design style with different widths for power vs signal traces.

incorrect.description: The fill_ratio is computed as filled_area_mm2 / outline_area_mm2 = 822.06 / 645.16 = 1.274. However, filled_area is the sum of both F.Cu (364.28mm2) and B.Cu (457.78mm2) fill areas, which correctly total 822.06mm2 for a dual-layer zone, but this sum exceeds the single-layer board footprint of 645.16mm2. The fill_ratio should be computed per-layer or against the total possible fill area (2 * 645.16 = 1290.32mm2), giving approximately 0.637 per-side. The current value misleadingly implies more than 100% fill of a single-layer area.

The via_in_pad analysis flags J2.NC2 and J1.NC1 NC (no-connect) pads as having nearby vias with same_net=false. The Qwiic connector (JST 1.0mm) has NC mechanical pads and the vias are placed nearby in the PCB layout. Since NC pads have no electrical connection, placing a via near them is not a DFM issue. The same_net=false flag and the NC designation indicate these are intentional layout choices, not violations. This is a false positive for via-in-pad classification.

findings.summary: Panelized PCB incorrectly reported as routing_complete=false due to NC pad nets spanning all 24 board copies; Panelized PCB statistics correctly show 1519 footprints on a 24-board panel (116.8 x 16...

correct.description: The panelized board multiplies the single board's 64 footprints across 24 panel instances (4-column x 6-row), yielding approximately 1519 footprints (24*64=1536 minus panel-specific exclusions). Board dimensions 116.8x168.6mm are consistent with a 4x6 grid of 25.4mm boards with V-score rails. 1848 vias (24*77=1848), 4488 track segments, and 24 GND zones all confirm 24x replication.

incorrect.description: The panelized board (24 copies on a panel) shows unrouted_count=5 and routing_complete=false. However, all 5 'unrouted' nets are intentionally-unconnected pad nets: unconnected-(J1-NC-PadNC1), unconnected-(J1-NC-PadNC2), unconnected-(J2-NC-PadNC1), unconnected-(J2-NC-PadNC2), and unconnected-(U1-NC-Pad4). These are NC pins that have 24 pads each (one per board copy) but no routing is expected. The single-board version correctly shows routing_complete=true. This is a false alarm in the panelized context.

findings.summary: Edge.Cuts reported as missing_required, but .GKO file is present and contains board outline; however the GKO file has an incorrect X2 FileFunction attribute; Drill classification correctly identifi...

correct.description: J3 is a 6-pin 0.1" PTH connector with 6 component holes + D3 (DNP) PTH LED with 2 holes = 8 total PTH component holes at 1.016mm. 77 via holes at 0.3mm match the PCB output. 4 NPTH mounting holes at 3.1mm for ST1-ST4 standoffs. Total 89 holes (77+8+4) are consistent across drill classification.

The gerber set correctly shows 2 copper layers (F.Cu/B.Cu), 9 gerber files (GTL, GBL, GTO, GBO, GTS, GBS, GTP, GBP, GKO), and 1 drill file. Three trace widths (0.2032, 0.4064, 0.5588mm) match the PCB output exactly. Total flashes=397 and draws=15388 are consistent with the board complexity.

incorrect.description: The Hardware directory contains SparkFun_AS7343_Qwiic.GKO which conventionally is Edge.Cuts/board outline. The file body contains a Profile aperture (TA.AperFunction,Profile) with a rectangular 25.4x25.4mm outline. However, the X2 TF.FileFunction attribute incorrectly declares it as 'Soldermask,Bot' (identical to the .GBS file). The analyzer correctly trusts the X2 attribute and does not recognize .GKO as Edge.Cuts, leading to missing_required=['Edge.Cuts']. This is a KiCad export inconsistency: the .GKO file has wrong X2 metadata. The analyzer behavior is technically correct but the underlying cause is a KiCad gerber export bug with the .GKO X2 attribute.

findings.summary: Production (panelized) gerber correctly shows 24x scale-up: 1848 vias, 192 component holes, 96 mounting holes, 2136 total holes; Gerber alignment is correct for both single board and panelized gerb...

correct.description: The panelized gerber has 1848 vias (24*77), 192 component holes (24*8), 96 NPTH mounting holes (24*4), totaling 2136 holes. SMD apertures=1710 (24 * ~71.25, consistent with 24 copies), via apertures=3696 (24*154). All are consistent with a 24-board panelization of the single board.

Both Hardware and Hardware_Production gerber sets report aligned=true with no alignment issues. Layer extents in the panelized set span approximately 102x159mm consistent with a 4x6 panel of 25.4mm boards. B.Paste extent of 0 for both sets is correct (no back-side SMD paste required since back components are all non-paste or the back paste layer is empty).
